title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following his discussion with Miss Emlyn, Poirot meets Elizabeth Whitaker, a guest at the Halloween party, to investigate the death of Joyce Reynolds. Poirot clarifies that he is conducting a private investigation at the request of a friend rather than through official police channels.

Whitaker provides a detailed account of the evening's events, specifically focusing on a moment during the "dragon hunt" game. She explains that she had stepped into the hallway to escape the heat of the festivities. While there, she observed Mrs. Dreckly emerging from a first-floor washroom carrying a large, heavy vase filled with flowers and autumn leaves. As Mrs. Dreckly stood on the staircase landing, she appeared to be staring intently toward the library door, which sits directly opposite the dining room door. Whitaker notes that Mrs. Dreckly seemed suddenly startled, as if she had seen something unexpected, such as a person appearing or a door opening. This shock caused Mrs. Dreckly to lose her grip, resulting in the vase shattering and spilling water across the hall floor.

Whitaker believes Mrs. Dreckly was frightened by something near the library, though Mrs. Dreckly did not express fear at the time. Poirot observes that since Mrs. Dreckly is not easily frightened, it is possible someone was in the library and retreated inside upon hearing the vase break, only to slip away after the guests departed. Whitaker concludes by recounting the sequence of party games, which included a broom race, a balloon race, a mirror trick, an obstacle race, a flour and sixpence game, dancing, and dinner.
